summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted body-based _ClientVersion value triggers multi-second regex backtracking before authorization and delays an unrelated health check handled by the same worker.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make the malicious request take several seconds and should delay a concurrent /parse/health request, demonstrating worker blocking and availability impact.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned Parse Server 8.6.57, built the reproduction image, and observed a baseline request in 7 ms, an attack request with a 100000-character _ClientVersion in 5235 ms, and a concurrent /parse/health request delayed to 5131 ms.
verification_method: The helper starts the vulnerable server, measures a short baseline request, then launches the malicious request and a concurrent health check. Reproduction succeeds only if the baseline stays below 500 ms while both the malicious request and the concurrent health check exceed 3000 ms.
evidence_produced_by_run: The crafted POST /parse/classes/TestObject with a 100000-character _ClientVersion took 5235 ms.
A concurrent GET /parse/health request took 5131 ms while the malicious parse was in progress.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
